title: The Fock-Darwin-Darboux system: eigenstates, information entropies and dispersion-like measures
authors: Ignacio Baena-Jimenez, Angel Ballesteros, Ivan Gutierrez-Sagredo
abstract: The Fock-Darwin (FD) quantum system describes the motion on the plane of a charged particle under the action of an isotropic oscillator potential together with a perpendicular constant magnetic field. When the isotropic oscillator is suppressed, the FD system leads to the Landau Hamiltonian with infinitely degenerate Landau levels. The Fock-Darwin-Darboux (FDD) system is the generalisation of the FD system to a particle moving on the Darboux III space, which is a conformally flat surface with non-constant negative curvature. We present a systematic study of some information-theoretic entropy and dispersion-like measures for these quantum systems. Since both systems are exactly solvable, analytical expressions for Shannon, Rényi and Tsallis entropies, among others, can be obtained. We show that for the FD system, its information-theoretic measures are formally the same as the ones for the harmonic oscillator, provided a modified effective frequency depending on the magnetic field is introduced. In the FDD case, the nonlinear nature of the underlying manifold precludes the existence of a simple closed form for the wave-function on momentum space, which is numerically analysed. We compare the numerical behaviour of the different entropy measures and we analyse the interplay arising in the FDD system between the curvature parameter and the magnetic field. In particular, it is shown that the Landau system on the Darboux III space has no infinitely degenerate Landau levels.
summary: This paper explores the information-theoretic properties of a quantum system moving on a curved surface under a magnetic field. It extends the well-known Fock-Darwin model to the Darboux III space, which has non-constant negative curvature. The authors calculate various entropies, such as Shannon and Tsallis, showing how the geometry of the space affects quantum uncertainty and the degeneracy of energy levels. The study highlights that the presence of curvature prevents the infinite degeneracy typically seen in the standard Landau levels.
main_problem: The study investigates information-theoretic entropy and dispersion-like measures for the Fock-Darwin-Darboux (FDD) system, which generalizes the Fock-Darwin system to a curved Darboux III space.
main_result: The authors derive analytical expressions for Shannon, Renyi, and Tsallis entropies for the FD system and provide numerical analysis for the FDD system, demonstrating that the curvature of the manifold breaks the infinite degeneracy of Landau levels.
method: The study utilizes exact solutions of the quantum Hamiltonian to obtain analytical expressions and employs numerical analysis for the momentum-space wave-functions in the FDD case.
model_or_system: The Fock-Darwin-Darboux (FDD) system, which describes a charged particle moving on a Darboux III space (a conformally flat surface with non-constant negative curvature) under the influence of a perpendicular constant magnetic field.
key_observables: Shannon entropy, Renyi entropy, Tsallis entropy, and dispersion-like measures.
important_parameters: Magnetic field strength, curvature parameter of the Darboux III space, and effective frequency.
main_assumptions: The system is assumed to be exactly solvable, and the underlying manifold is assumed to be a Darboux III space.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the FD and FDD systems, provides analytical derivations for entropy measures in the FD case, presents numerical analysis for the FDD case due to the lack of closed-form momentum-space wave-functions, and concludes with a comparison of entropy behaviors and the effect of curvature on Landau levels.
